questions.question: Prove that the Bayes' theorem is equivalent to the conditional probability formula P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B).
A coin is flipped 3 times, and the results are 2 heads and 1 tail. What is the probability that the tail occurred on the first flip? Use Bayes' theorem to solve this problem.

A medical test has a true positive rate of 95%, false positive rate of 5%, and prior probability of disease of 0.1. If a patient tests positive, what is the posterior probability of the patient having the disease? Use Bayes' theorem to solve this problem.
